channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: The VISION-AI Trial: protocol for a pragmatic randomized controlled non-inferiority trial comparing artificial intelligence-guided colonoscopy to pancolonic chromoendoscopy for neoplasia detection in adults with colorectal inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Current guidelines recommend pancolonic chromoendoscopy (pCE) over white light endoscopy (WLE) alone for colorectal neoplasia (CRN) detection in individuals with inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD). ...

item.title: Artificial intelligence assessment of endoscopic severity and extent: A machine learning approach to continuous evaluation of endoscopic inflammation in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The endoscopy subscore is a therapeutic endpoint in ulcerative colitis trials but is limited by reader variability and inability to fully capture the degree of inflammation across the colon. We dev...

item.title: Artificial Intelligence in the Nutritional Management of Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Scoping Review.
item.description: Journal of multidisciplinary healthcare - Review - Diet is closely associated with the onset, progression, and prognosis of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). In the absence of specific dietary and nutritional guidelines, nutritional management for ...

item.title: Lowering the C-Reactive Protein (CRP) Threshold Improves Risk Stratification in Acute Ulcerative Colitis: A Propensity-Matched Analysis.
item.description: Alimentary pharmacology & therapeutics - Real-world data demonstrate that patients hospitalised with non-acute severe UC (NASUC) experience intravenous corticosteroid (IVCS) failure rates approaching those of ASUC, yet no dedicated manage...

item.title: Trained immunity: An unexplored component of IBD pathogenesis.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Trained immunity (TI) describes the capability of the innate immune system and its tissue-resident cells to acquire long-lasting functional adaptations following an initial inflammatory or microbia...

item.title: Meta-Analysis of DNA methylation and gut microbiome data in preterm birth reveals epigenetic and microbial biomarkers for early diagnosis and probiotic-based intervention.
item.description: Archives of physiology and biochemistry - Background: Globally, preterm birth continues to be a major contributor to neonatal morbidity and mortality. Developing early diagnostics and focused interventions requires an understanding of the ...

item.title: Safety and Pharmacokinetics of Single and Multiple Ascending Doses of Olamkicept up to 2400 mg in Healthy Men: Phase I, Randomized, Placebo-Controlled, and Double-Blind Trial.
item.description: Clinical and translational science - RCT - Olamkicept is a first-in-class fully human fusion protein that selectively inhibits trans interleukin (IL)-6 signaling. Previous phase I clinical trials demonstrated that single doses up to 750 mg ...

item.title: Signal mining and analysis of adverse events associated with Isotretinoin: A 20-Year real-world pharmacovigilance study based on FAERS and EudraVigilance databases.
item.description: PloS one - To systematically characterize the post-marketing safety signals of isotretinoin using real-world data from the U.S. FDA Adverse Event Reporting System (FAERS), with independent external validation...

item.title: Sensitivity and specificity of diagnostic modalities to diagnose CMV in coexisting IBD in patients hospitalized with acute colitis: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Cytomegalovirus (CMV), a common infection, can reactivate and cause CMV-associated colitis in immunosuppressed people, like those with known inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Diagnosis of CMV colit...

item.title: Characteristics and outcomes of small-bowel vascular lesions and nonvascular lesions in patients with suspected small-bowel bleeding detected by deep enteroscopy: a single-center experience in Taiwan.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Small-bowel vascular lesions are a major cause of suspected small-bowel bleeding (SSBB), which poses considerable challenges in clinical practice. Hence, this study aimed to identify the clinical c...

item.title: Clinical indications and outcomes of pediatric gastrointestinal endoscopy in a resource-limited healthcare system: Findings from the first multicenter study.
item.description: SAGE open medicine - The objective of this study was to provide the first multicenter evaluation of pediatric gastrointestinal endoscopy in Palestine, specifically characterizing clinical indications, sedation practice...

item.title: Underevaluation of bowel urgency in clinical care: patient experiences and outcomes in ulcerative colitis: a retrospective cohort study.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Bowel urgency increases the burden of disease in patients with ulcerative colitis (UC). It is unclear how urgency impacts UC-related outcomes independent of other hallmark symptoms such as rectal b...

item.title: The association between artificial light at night and inflammatory bowel disease incidence, surgery, and health services utilization: population-based observational studies.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The urban environment increases the risk of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Specific environmental exposures involved in IBD etiology remain unknown. We examined the association between outdoor a...

item.title: Absolute risk of malignancy by treatment in patients with Crohn's disease compared to the general population: a nationwide population-based cohort study.
item.description: The American journal of gastroenterology - Real-world data quantifying absolute cancer risk in Crohn's disease (CD) by treatment status are lacking but are essential for patient counselling. We linked nationwide Swedish register data and as...

item.title: Relationship between sacroiliac joint involvement and intestinal disease activity in newly diagnosed Crohn's disease.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Axial musculoskeletal involvement represents an important component among the extraintestinal manifestations of Crohn's disease (CD). Sacroiliac joint (SIJ) involvement can be detected at an early ...

item.title: The Association Between Vitiligo and Uveitis: A Large Population-Based Study.
item.description: International journal of dermatology - Vitiligo is a common acquired immune-mediated skin disease, often associated with other systemic inflammatory conditions. However, a clear association between vitiligo and uveitis, a vision-threate...

item.title: Circulating multi-omic signature of disease severity and cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis.
item.description: Hepatology communications - Primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) is a chronic cholestatic liver disease often associated with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) that can progress to cirrhosis or cholangiocarcinoma (CCA), both c...

item.title: Association between autoimmune diseases and male reproductive health: A mendelian randomization study.
item.description: Science progress - ObjectiveThe relationship between autoimmune diseases (ADs) and male reproductive health is easily neglected. Epidemiological studies indicated that multiple ADs may adversely affect male reproduct...

item.title: Integrative Analysis of Genetic and Cellular Interactions Connecting Inflammatory Bowel Disease and Primary Biliary Cholangitis.
item.description: Clinics and research in hepatology and gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and primary biliary cholangitis (PBC) have been reported to coexist in some patients, but the extent of this association remains to be further clarified. Clarifying...

item.title: Multi-omics Mendelian randomization and experimental validation identify PRKAB1 as a regulator of phosphatidylcholine metabolism in IBD.
item.description: Naunyn-Schmiedeberg's archives of pharmacology - Phosphatidylcholines (PCs) are implicated in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), yet the genetic and pathophysiological mechanisms underlying phosphatidylcholine metabolism in IBD remain poorly chara...
